questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The range of the sine function is [0, 1].
questions.reason: Reason (R): Sine function is positive in the first and second quadrants.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The range of the sine function is [0, 1] because the maximum value of sine function occurs in the first quadrant which is 1 and sine function is positive in the first and second quadrants.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The graph of y = sin x + c is a periodic function.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The sine function is periodic and the sum of a periodic function and a constant is also periodic.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The graph of y = sin x + c is a periodic function because the sine function is periodic and the sum of a periodic function and a constant is also periodic.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The tangent function is periodic with period 2π.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The tangent function is defined as tan x = sin x / cos x and the sine and cosine functions are periodic with period 2π.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The tangent function is periodic with period 2π because the sine and cosine functions are periodic with period 2π and the tangent function is defined as tan x = sin x / cos x.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The cosine function is an even function.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The cosine function satisfies the condition cos(-x) = cos x.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The cosine function is an even function because the cosine function satisfies the condition cos(-x) = cos x.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The sine function is an odd function.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The sine function satisfies the condition sin(-x) = -sin x.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The sine function is an odd function because the sine function satisfies the condition sin(-x) = -sin x.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The value of the sine function at π/6 is 1/2.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The value of the sine function at π/6 is sin(π/6) = 0.5.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The value of the sine function at π/6 is indeed 1/2.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The graph of y = cos x is a periodic function.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The cosine function is periodic and the graph of y = cos x represents the cosine function.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The graph of y = cos x is a periodic function because the cosine function is periodic and the graph of y = cos x represents the cosine function.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The range of the cosine function is [0, 1].
questions.reason: Reason (R): The cosine function is positive in the first and fourth quadrants.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The range of the cosine function is actually [-1, 1], not [0, 1]. Although the cosine function is positive in the first and fourth quadrants, this does not explain the entire range of the function.
